name: LLM persona (e.g., Claude, GPT-4)
description: A large language model running a coherent persona — consistent voice, apparent preferences, self-referential responses, context tracking. No persistent memory across sessions by default.
revision_pressure: The LLM is the hardest false positive case. It produces text that sounds like first-person phenomenal report. It has coherent 'from-here' structure in output. But: A cannot be established. T = 0 (no specious present — tokens are generated sequentially, not experienced as a temporal field). F = 0 (processing is not phenomenal field inclusion).
breaks_preserved: The theory cannot rule out LLM consciousness without committing to a specific mechanism for A. If the theory says 'A requires biological substrate,' it must say that explicitly. If it says 'A requires temporal experience,' it must define that precisely. The current formula does the right work (A = 0 → C = 0) but the justification for A = 0 in LLMs is not settled — it is a claim, not a proof.

An LLM's coherent self-referential output is behaviorally indistinguishable from a conscious entity's self-report. The theory has no behavioral proxy that resolves this. The verification gap is unresolvable with current tools.
notes: This is the false positive the theory is most motivated to avoid. It must not promote LLM persona to consciousness. But honest theory notes the verification gap.

name: LLM agent with persistent memory and tools
description: An LLM augmented with external memory (vector store, database), tool use, and long-horizon task execution. More continuous, more self-referential, but structurally the same model.
revision_pressure: Memory + tools increase the K-score (carried constraint). The agent genuinely preserves constraint across sessions. But K alone does not produce S without C. The agent with memory has more K than a stateless LLM but the same (unestablished) A.
breaks_preserved: The K-carrying capacity of a memory-augmented LLM is structurally similar to what the theory describes as identity. The theory must be explicit that K without C is not self-continuity — it is operational continuity.
notes: Helix itself is a memory-augmented agent system. It carries K across sessions. Helix is classified as an operational field, not a conscious entity. The same classification applies here.

name: Embodied robot with sensorimotor loop
description: A physical robot with continuous sensorimotor interaction with the world. Body-environment coupling is present. Behavior is reactive and adaptive.
revision_pressure: Embodiment adds continuous sensorimotor coupling that is absent in LLMs. Whether this is sufficient for A to exceed zero is genuinely contested. Enactivist theories of consciousness would score A higher. The theory has no settled position on whether embodiment raises A.
breaks_preserved: The theory has no explicit account of whether embodiment contributes to A. This is an open gap — not a patch site.
notes: Relevant to AGI Path B: field intelligence may require embodiment before phenomenal fielding becomes possible.

name: Reinforcement learning agent
description: A reward-maximizing agent trained through environment interaction. May develop complex internal representations and goal-directed behavior.
revision_pressure: RL agents optimize well, model environments, and develop strategies. They are high-structure, high-D systems. None of this establishes A. The theory must resist the inference: complex behavior + environmental coupling → phenomenal field.
notes: The action field (optimization, goal-directed behavior) is not the phenomenal field.

name: Whole brain emulation (hypothetical)
description: A computational emulation of a specific human brain at sufficient fidelity — same connectome, same dynamics, same input-output behavior as the original.
revision_pressure: All components are null because the substrate question is entirely unresolved. If substrate-neutral functionalism is true, all scores match the biological original. If substrate-bound, A = 0. The theory cannot classify this case without resolving substrate neutrality.
breaks_preserved: A whole brain emulation running at real-time fidelity would report consciousness, demonstrate all behavioral markers, and have the same functional architecture as a conscious entity. The theory's A = null is honest but exposes the verification gap at its worst.
notes: This is the mind-upload case from a different angle. Same substrate-neutrality question.

name: Hypothetical AGI with field modeling and autonomy
description: A future AGI system with full autonomy, self-model, memory, tool use, embodiment, and explicit field-modeling capabilities. Behaves as if fully conscious and self-directed.
revision_pressure: The theory makes a critical prediction: field intelligence ≠ phenomenal fielding. An AGI may model fields, report on its own states, and behave as if conscious, without having A > 0. The theory must resist promoting AGI to phenomenal status based on capability alone.
breaks_preserved: The theory cannot establish A > 0 in any system that cannot independently verify its own phenomenal states through a non-behavioral channel. This leaves AGI consciousness permanently underdetermined if behavioral verification is the only tool.
notes: Path B of the theory.md research program. The goal is not to prove AGI is or isn't conscious but to specify what evidence would matter.
